howItWorks.intro: This booklet page imposition calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Signatures = ceil(Total Pages / Pages per Signature)
Sheets per Signature = Pages per Signature / 4
Blank Pages = (Signatures x Pages per Sig) - Total Pages
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a signature in printing?
faq.answer: A signature is a large sheet printed on both sides, then folded and trimmed to create a section of consecutive pages. A 16-page signature prints 8 pages per side on one sheet. Multiple signatures are gathered and bound together to form the book.

faq.question: Why must booklet page counts be multiples of 4?
faq.answer: Because each sheet of paper creates 4 pages (front and back, two sides of the fold). Saddle-stitched booklets must have page counts divisible by 4. If your content does not fill evenly, blank pages are added at the end or distributed throughout.
